channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump Administration Withheld Details of Iranian Attacks on U.S. Forces
item.description: The Trump administration has been accused of withholding critical information about Iranian attacks on U.S. military forces, according to a report by The New York Times. While the Pentagon publicly di...

item.title: Ryanair Reports 34% Drop in Profits Amid Rising Fuel Costs and Fare Cuts
item.description: Ryanair, the budget airline group, has reported a significant decline in its after-tax profits for the three months ending June, with a 34 per cent drop to €538 million. This downturn is primarily att...

item.title: Trump's Vision for Hosting the 2026 World Cup Demonstrates American Excellence
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, executive director of the White House Task Force on the 2026 FIFA World Cup, highlights President Trump’s pivotal role in organizing the event. The tournament, held across the U.S., M...

item.title: Canoe Capsizes in Jigawa: Six Dead, 15 Rescued
item.description: A canoe accident in Jigawa State's Ringim Local Government Area claimed six lives and rescued 15 people, with several others still missing. The incident occurred on July 20, 2026, around 4 pm in Malam...

item.title: Tanzania promotes natural heritage through UNESCO meeting
item.description: Tanzania has leveraged the 48th UNESCO General Assembly to showcase its diverse natural heritage, emphasizing the importance of preserving ecosystems and cultural landscapes. The country's participati...

item.title: Soldiers' salary review sparks debate on adequacy amid rising costs in Nigeria
item.description: The Nigerian government has doubled the minimum monthly salary for soldiers from N49,000 to N100,000, but civil society organizations (CSOs) and advocacy groups argue that this increase does not match...

item.title: Philippine and Chinese forces clash near Ayungin Shoal
item.description: A Filipino soldier was injured during a confrontation with Chinese Coast Guard (CCG) personnel near Ayungin Shoal in the West Philippine Sea. The incident occurred on July 20, 2026, when CCG personnel...

item.title: Andy Burnham appointed UK PM amid economic challenges and reform pledges
item.description: Andy Burnham has been appointed as the new Prime Minister of the UK following the resignation of Keir Starmer. In his inaugural address, Burnham emphasized the need for Britain to regain stability, ad...

item.title: Senate Reform Debates Continue in Canadian Political Discourse
item.description: The article compiles letters to the editor discussing various political, social, and economic issues in Canada. One letter emphasizes the urgent need for Senate reform, referencing the 1992 Charlottet...

item.title: Australian government's response to Islamophobia report lacks detailed action
item.description: The Albanese government's response to Aftab Malik's Islamophobia report has been criticized for its lack of concrete measures despite acknowledging the issue's severity. The report, tabled ten months ...

item.title: News Article: JSON Response for a Technical Article
item.description: The article discusses the creation of a JSON response containing metadata and content for a news article. It outlines the structure of the JSON fields, including the title, summary, and tags. The arti...

item.title: The Pressure of Track Competition: High School Runners' Struggles for College Scholarships
item.description: High school track athletes face immense pressure to excel at national meets, where financial sacrifices and intense competition drive their pursuit of college scholarships. Events like the Asics Carol...

item.title: Lesotho's Snake Conservation Park and the 30-Year Struggle Against Witchcraft Accusations and Government Inaction
item.description: Nkhooa Molahlehi, a Lesotho native, has spent three decades establishing a snake conservation park under a sandstone cliff, facing accusations of witchcraft and bureaucratic delays. After 20 years of ...

item.title: DMK MLA Arrested for Alleged Derogatory Remarks About Tamil Nadu CM
item.description: The article reports the arrest of Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) MLA GV Markandayan for making allegedly derogatory remarks about Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin. The arrest followed a complain...

item.title: Senate Grants Subpoenas for Sara Duterte's Financial Records in Impeachment Trial
item.description: The Senate impeachment court has issued subpoenas for the bank and tax records of Vice President Sara Duterte and her husband, Mans Carpio, as part of the ongoing investigation into alleged unexplaine...

item.title: CCTV Footage Deleted in Murder Trial of Former Police Officer
item.description: The murder trial of former Nairobi Central Police Station Officer Commanding Station (OCS) Samson Talaam and five co-accused has begun at Kibera High Court. The Director of Public Prosecution (DPP), R...

item.title: Bajabulise Thela Crowned Miss Universe South Africa 2026, Set to Represent at Global Pageant
item.description: Bajabulise Thela, a 23-year-old marketing graduate and qualified flight attendant from Mpumalanga, has been crowned Miss Universe South Africa 2026. She will represent the country at the 75th Miss Uni...

item.title: GOP Infighting Threatens Trump's Legislative Agenda as Hardliners Risk Sabotage

item.title: UIF outlines the main reasons unemployment benefit claims are rejected and explains the appeals process

item.title: Simba and Yanga to Face Off in October 19 League Match at Uhuru Stadium
